calendar.matters:
Petition Of Beneficiary Diane G. Garcia, To Remove And/Or Disqualify Successor Trustees, Patricia G. Cuellar And Dolores A. Chavez To Act As A Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-27-2023 Off Calendar

Petition Of Beneficiary Diane G. Garcia, To Remove And/Or Disqualify Successor Trustees, Patricia G. Cuellar And Dolores A. Chavez To Act As A Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-19-2022 Continued To Mar-27-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Ritzinger, via Zoom, for Diane Garcia, Petitioner; Lon Lazar, via Zoom, for Patricia Cuellar, Respondent; Dolores Chavez, via Zoom, in propria persona, co-Trustee.

At the query of the court, Mr. Ritzinger reports that there has been no movement since the previous calling and makes an oral request for the court to order Ms. Chavez to file a response in thirty days. Ms. Chavez reports of continuing to work on finding an attorney and requests a continuance accordingly. Mr. Lazar reports on the ongoing, diligent efforts regarding the fraudulent financial action in this matter and that he is working with a local F.B.I. agent. Mr. Lazar advises that he received a letter from Bank of America advising that they had concluded their investigation regarding the fraud. At the court's query, Mr. Lazar advises that he believes his practice insurance may cover the amount presumed lost due to the scam. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to this matter with Mr. Ritzinger requesting an evidentiary hearing and reiterates his request that Ms. Chavez be ordered to file a response within thirty (30) days. The court offers its position that an evidentiary hearing will not help resolve this matter.

After additional discussion, the subject matter is continued to Mar-27-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

Petition Of Beneficiary Diane G. Garcia, To Remove And/Or Disqualify Successor Trustees, Patricia G. Cuellar And Dolores A. Chavez To Act As A Trustee
Off Calendar Nov-07-2022 Continued To Dec-19-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar Aug-03-2022 Continued To Nov-07-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Of Beneficiary Diane G. Garcia, To Remove And/Or Disqualify Successor Trustees, Patricia G. Cuellar And Dolores A. Chavez To Act As A Trustee
Off Calendar Jul-25-2022 Continued To Aug-03-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Of Beneficiary Diane G. Garcia, To Remove And/Or Disqualify Successor Trustees, Patricia G. Cuellar And Dolores A. Chavez To Act As A Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-25-2022 Continued To Jul-25-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Ritzinger, via Zoom, for Diane Garcia, Petitioner; Lon Lazar, via Zoom, for Patricia Cuellar, Successor Trustee.

Mr. Ritzinger reports that the real property has been sold but that he has not been provided with any information regarding a proposed distribution and that he has not yet been provided with an accounting. Mr. Lazar offers testimony regarding his client's position and reports that he can provide an accounting within forty-five days. The court advises counsel of the importance of transparency. Messrs. Ritzinger and Lazar offer additional testimony in support of their respective positions. The court will not order an accounting at this time but advises the parties to be ready to discuss the subject at the next calling of this matter.

After additional discussion, the Petition of Beneficiary Diane Garcia, etc., is continued to Jul-25-2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Gomez. Not reported.

Petition Of Beneficiary Diane G. Garcia, To Remove And/Or Disqualify Successor Trustees, Patricia G. Cuellar And Dolores A. Chavez To Act As A Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-26-2022 Continued To Apr-25-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Of Beneficiary Diane G. Garcia, To Remove And/Or Disqualify Successor Trustees, Patricia G. Cuellar And Dolores A. Chavez To Act As A Trustee
Off Calendar Sep-29-2021 Continued To Jan-26-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Ritzinger, via ZOOM, for Diane Garcia, Petitioner; Lon Lazar, via ZOOM, for Patricia Cuellar, via ZOOM, Successor Trustee.

The court queries counsel regarding the status of the appraisal of the real property. Mr. Ritzinger reports that the appraisal is complete but that the results of the appraisal are pending. The court discusses the agreement between the parties in the matter and states that it appears as though the parties need some time for the process to unfold. Mr. Ritzinger agrees with the court. The court then proposes a four (4) month continuance. Counsel agrees.

The Petition of Beneficiary, etc., is continued to Jan-26-2022 9:00 am in Department 204. If the matter settles, the parties are instructed to file a joint statement regarding settlement by Jan-17-2022

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.

Petition Of Beneficiary Diane G. Garcia, To Remove And/Or Disqualify Successor Trustees, Patricia G. Cuellar And Dolores A. Chavez To Act As A Trustee
Off Calendar Aug-04-2021 Continued To Sep-29-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Ritzinger appeared by zoom for and with Diane Garcia (Petitioner) by zoom.
Lon Lazar appeared by zoom for and with Patty Cuellar (Co-Trustee) by zoom.
Dolores Chavez (Co-Trustee) appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

Mr. Lazar stated that there is no evidence to warrant removal of his Client and that if the Petitioner wishes to proceed with removal, he would ask the Court for an evidentiary hearing. Mr. Lazar also stated that they have had some discussion, asked the Court to send the parties to mediation and stated that there isn't much in the trust, just a piece of real property. Mr. Ritzinger stated that perhaps he would agree to mediation at a later time, raised the issue of nothing having been done with regard to administration of the trust, and stated that there was an indication by prior Counsel that Patricia would resign, but nothing was received, Mr. Ritzinger also stated that the case has been pending since March, that there has been no fruitful effort to do what needs to be done to administer the trust, that he requested some proof that Patricia has the financial ability to buy the other beneficiaries out and that his request has been completely ignored. In addition, Mr. Ritzinger stated that Patricia had been living in the property by herself, that she has moved her adult children into the property and that his Client has no access to the property. Mr. Lazar stated that there is significant concern about the validity of the 4th amendment to the trust, that his Client has decided not to resign and is amenable to winding up administration of trust and deal with the issue of the property, and that if his Client is unable to obtain financing, they will have to sell the property. The Court signed an order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than September 15, 2021. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by September 20, 2021. The Petition to Remove and/or Disqualify Successor Trustees, etc. is continued to and the Parties are to return on September 29, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. Counsel are to obtain a copy of the mediation order from the Court's website.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: L. Maloney. Not reported.
